summary: A fortified castle-like structure with a rectangular footprint featuring two tall corner towers with overhanging roofs, a central main building with stepped roof elements, and a symmetrical layout. The structure uses wood and stonebrick materials with regular window patterns and is surrounded by a stone foundation base.
rebuild_hints: Build two tall rectangular towers at front corners, each approximately 3 blocks wide and 12+ blocks tall
Create main rectangular body between towers with 3 visible floor levels
Add horizontal stonebrick bands at regular intervals across wood walls
Place small square windows in 4x3 grid pattern on front and side faces
Construct stepped/tiered roof with multiple dark wood slab layers creating overhang effect
Add corner tower roofs with extended overhangs (3-4 block projection)
Build stone foundation base with regular block pattern around entire perimeter
Ensure bilateral symmetry along the central vertical axis
Central roof section should be higher than corner tower roofs
Use dark wood for all roof elements and trim details
uncertainties: Exact interior layout not visible from exterior views
Precise window frame material (appears to be dark wood or dark stone)
Depth of roof overhang may vary by 1-2 blocks
Whether central section has additional height variation not clearly visible from all angles
Exact number of interior floors vs. exterior floor markers
